tables.title: The Five Blast Injury Mechanisms
rows: Blast pressure wave at air-tissue interfaces
TM rupture, blast lung, GI perforation/hemorrhage, air embolism
Fragments and debris propelled by the blast
Penetrating trauma — the most common cause of death/injury
Burns, crush (collapse), smoke inhalation, exacerbated illness

tables.title: Bombing Casualty Flow — Planning Points
rows: Cohort in fast-track; rapid assessment; don't fill resus bays
Critical casualties via EMS after extrication
Protected resuscitation/OR capacity; trauma teams ready
Delayed presenters (blast lung, GI injury, anxiety)
Observation pathways; return precautions for discharged
